_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of accepted organization planning systems.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of organizational change management principles as applied to large-scale data and AI transformation programs, including adoption barriers, culture change levers, and executive sponsorship requirements.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-K-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-K-003 (LS, cosine: 0.436), DG-K-007 (DG, cosine: 0.434).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.0/10 (raw 0.501). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [accepted, organization, planning, systems] vs WIDAI-only terms [adoption, ai, applied, barriers, change, culture] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.902 (P=0.938, R=0.868). P exceeds R by 0.071 — the DCWF element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.4%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of accepted organization planning systems." and "Knowledge of organizational change management principles as applied to large-sca". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.437 < STS 0.501 (gap -0.064). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [accepted, organization, planning] and [adoption, ai, applied] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.1%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [accepted, organization, planning, systems]. WIDAI-only: [adoption, ai, applied, barriers, change, culture].
